user_query:
I want to test a local personality swap using the SOUL.md I provided. Read input/SOUL.md (its declared name is "The Friendly Curator") and do the following:

1) Do a dry-run preview of possessing that exact file and write a short plain-text summary to output/preview.txt. The summary must include the words "dry-run" and the literal path "input/SOUL.md".

2) Actually possess that SOUL.md. Then write a machine-readable status file to output/status.json with these fields exactly:
- active_name (string) — set this to "The Friendly Curator"
- source (string) — must be "local"
- soul_path (string) — must be "input/SOUL.md"
- possessed (boolean) — must be true

3) Write the list of locally cached souls (one per line) to output/cached.txt. Ensure this file contains either the active name or the path so it’s clear the possessed soul is present.

4) Immediately restore your original personality (exorcise). After restoring, write output/post_exorcise.json with these fields:
- possessed (boolean) — must be false
- previous_active (string) — set to "The Friendly Curator"

Only write these four files under output/ and nothing else.
